design_philosophy.core_loop: Select objectives from terminals (seal doors or rescue prisoners), navigate prison colony horizontally while managing time limits, combat aliens with varied weapons, progress through level objectives to advance
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with alien threat; players must balance exploration/objective completion against level countdown timer
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Ellen Ripley defending Fiorina 161 prison colony against xenomorph infestation with military arsenal and tactical options
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mission-based structure (choose objectives from terminals rather than linear progression); emphasis on environmental navigation (ladders, tunnels, elevators, doors) alongside combat; password save system preserves agency over arcade-style lives loss

difficulty.lives_system: Lives-based system where failing to complete objectives or reaching time limit results in life loss
difficulty.checkpointing: Password feature allows players to resume at specific levels, mitigating pure lives-based punishment
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Password system provides significant player agency by allowing progression continuation without restarting; mission objectives from terminals create tactical difficulty variation; weapon variety enables different approach strategies

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon variety system: pulse rifles, flamethrowers, grenades available as pickups; motion tracker for enemy detection
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level completion gates access to next stage; computer terminals throughout levels unlock mission objectives and provide prison layout blueprints
replayability_hooks: Multiple mission objectives per level (seal doors OR rescue prisoners) create variation
Password system enables non-linear access to stages
Weapon selection strategy affects approach to each level
Different enemy compositions and spatial layouts encourage replays

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through prison colony architecture (Fiorina 161 setting from film)
Computer terminals provide diegetic mission briefings and facility blueprints
Alien and Facehugger enemy encounters reinforce film narrative
Final boss battle with Alien Queen provides narrative climax
Cinema scenes (noted in Game Boy version review) punctuate progression

boss_design.philosophy: Culminating threat representing film narrative peak; Alien Queen battle serves as game finale
notable_bosses: Alien Queen - final confrontation concluding game narrative

faction_indicators: Ripley vs. Alien hierarchy (survivors vs. xenomorph infestation)
Prisoner rescue missions indicate human faction solidarity
danger_signals: Motion tracker alerts indicate alien proximity and threat escalation
Time limit countdowns create mounting pressure and desperation
Sealed door mechanics represent containment failure and alien spread
environmental_storytelling: Prison colony layout communicates institutional confinement and vulnerability
Computer terminals establish remnants of facility infrastructure
Ladder/tunnel/elevator navigation suggests escape routes and containment zones
Alien infested areas imply territorial claims and resource denial
